rulings.Case Title: BRYANT FU ET AL VS. TINA YAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Sever And Consolidate Thai Ming Chiu'S Cross-Action From Case No Cgc-17-556769 And Consolidating It With Cgc 19-579664
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 9, 2022 line 1. PLAINTIFFS BRYANT FU AND CRYSTAL LEI'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO SEVER AND CONSOLIDATE THAI MING CHIU'S CROSS-ACTION FROM CASE NO. CGC-17-556769 AND CONSOLIDATING IT WITH CGC-19-579664 is OFF CALENDAR. Motion is premature as it was filed before third amended cross-complaint was filed on March 7. Plaintiff can refile the motion now that third amended cross-complaint is filed. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: LADY BENJAMIN CANNON VS. CHARLES JADALLAH ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Lady Benjamin Cannon'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For A Protective Order
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 9, 2022 line 6. PLAINTIFF LADY BENJAMIN CANNON'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR A PROTECTIVE ORDER is continued to March 11, 2022. No later than 9:00 a.m. on March 9, 2022 Plaintiff shall file a supplemental declaration identifying each document (by title, description, etc.) potentially subject to "confidentiality/attorney's eyes only" designation and the request for production such document is responsive to. No later than March 9, 2022 by 5:00 p.m. parties to meet and confer regarding the designation and the form of the Protective Order. No later than March 10, 2022 by 9:00 a.m. parties to file a supplemental brief identifying remaining items in dispute; if parties cannot agree on a joint brief, each party may file a separate brief. Briefs shall only contain identification of the document and the request for production such document is responsive to. If after meet and confer efforts any items remain in dispute (other than the form of order) Plaintiff's counsel is to appear in person on March 11, 2022 at 9:30 a.m. and bring copies of every document in dispute. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: KIM KLAUSNER, TRUSTEE UNDER THE KIM KLAUSNER VS. AIMEE TSAO ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 9, 2022 line 11. DEFENDANTS AIMEE TSAO AND LORIN TSAO'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is continued to March 14, 2022. The Court will take judicial notice of all exhibits submitted by the defendant for the purpose of establishing their existence and their legal effect. The Court also finds that the prior Order and Judgment determined that Van Buskirk was a tenant at all times relevant to the prior action and that any issues relevant to the change of the tenancy per the Trust to a license prior to the hearing in the earlier case should have and could have been raised in that action. In light of this ruling and findings, Plaintiff shall file a declaration attaching a proposed amendment complaint including allegations made in good faith stating a cause of action for unlawful detainer under properly initiated Ellis Act. All amendments shall be in bold font. NO further argument is allowed. Declaration shall be filed and served no later than March 10, 2022 by 9:00 a.m. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: C&C HARGENS, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY VS. JIN PARK ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 9, 2022 line 12. DEFENDANT JIN PARK'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained in part and overruled in part. Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend for plaintiff to allege in good faith that that Plaintiff provided notice to defendant of whether the date of withdrawal was extended to one year and the new date of withdrawal. Demurrer is otherwise overruled, Complaint read as a whole states that there was no written rental agreement. Notifications provided to the tenant are compliance with SFRO and Ellis Act. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
